versions.label: LSB
versions.text: "Truly, truly, I say to you, if you ask the Father for anything in My name, He will give it to you. Until now you have asked for nothing in My name; ask and you will receive, so that your joy may be made full."

context_lines: In **John 16**, Jesus continues preparing the disciples for His death, resurrection, departure, and the coming of the Spirit. Its promises about prayer and joy are part of that transition into the new covenant reality of life in His name.
explanation_lines: The surface reading people take from this is straightforward — ask anything, in Jesus' name, and you will receive it, and your joy will be complete. It sounds like the broadest promise in the New Testament on prayer.

Two things settle it. First, "in My name" carries the same meaning established in John 14:13-14 — asking in alignment with His character, authority, and purposes, not a verbal formula guaranteeing delivery.
